title: pouteria chrysophylloides
intro: Pouteria chrysophylloides, also known as the yellow-leaf pouteria, is a plant species that belongs to the family Sapotaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark, leaves, and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
